title: Hot Water Beach Review
meta_desc: Discover Hot Water Beach in Coromandel, NZ. A unique family experience where geothermal water meets the sea. Free admission, suitable for all ages.
intro: Hot Water Beach in Coromandel is a must-visit destination for families. This natural wonder allows you to dig your own hot pool on the beach, making it an unforgettable experience. With free admission and a unique concept, it's an attractive option for families looking for something new and exciting.

sections.heading: What to Expect
sections.body:
Hot Water Beach is genuinely one-of-a-kind. At low tide, geothermal water seeps through the sand, and you can hire a spade for $5 to dig your own hot pool right on the beach. The water can reach 64°C in places, so it's essential to dig carefully and mix in cold seawater to achieve a comfortable temperature.

The experience is suitable for all ages, from 0+, making it an excellent option for families with young children. However, be aware that the hot water can be a hazard if not managed properly, so close supervision is necessary.
list: Mix in cold seawater for a comfortable temperature

sections.heading: Value for Money and Insider Tips
sections.body:
With free admission, Hot Water Beach offers excellent value for money. The only cost is the $5 spade hire, which is a small price to pay for this unique experience. To make the most of your visit, check the tide times in advance and plan your visit accordingly, as the hot water is only accessible 2 hours either side of low tide.

It's also a good idea to arrive early, as the beach can get crowded, especially during peak season. Don't forget to bring towels, sunscreen, and a picnic lunch to make the most of your day.
list: Bring towels, sunscreen, and a picnic lunch

sections.heading: Best Time to Visit and Booking
sections.body:
Hot Water Beach is open all year round, but the best time to visit is during the summer months when the weather is warm and sunny. There's no need to book in advance, as admission is free and spade hire is available on a first-come, first-served basis.

However, it's a good idea to check the official website for any updates or closures before you go. You can also find information on tide times, spade hire, and other essential details to plan your visit.

sections.heading: Downsides for Families
sections.body:
While Hot Water Beach is an excellent destination for families, there are a few downsides to consider. The hot water can be a hazard if not managed properly, and the beach can get crowded during peak season. Additionally, the experience is weather-dependent, and strong winds or rough seas can make it difficult to enjoy the hot pools.

However, with some planning and preparation, these downsides can be mitigated, and you can still have a fantastic time with your family.
